mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor demand and rising consumer income, which drives equity valuations higher through increased corporate earnings expectations and consumer spending. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days when PAYEMS shows consistent month-over-month expansion.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 133786k as of Jan 2011 indicates post-recession recovery momentum
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market normalization
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive PAYEMS growth fails to produce positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 1.0% for three consecutive months, the zero-lower-bound monetary regime signals extreme accommodation that suppresses risk-free rates and forces capital into equities as a yield-seeking mechanism. This threshold-triggered signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days when activated.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 3.26% as of Jan 2011 indicates post-crisis normalization phase
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS < 1.0% for 3+ months, 60-day forward returns are negative in > 40% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand and consumer confidence, which compresses equity risk premiums and produces negative S&P 500 returns. This interaction-driven mechanism requires both series to decline simultaneously to activate the bearish signal over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 2% month-over-month in same month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 357013M and HOUST at 2004k as of Dec 2010
regime_hint: late-cycle demand deceleration with housing weakness
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and HOUST decline > 2% simultaneously, 20-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above 0.5% month-over-month signals expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side strength, which supports corporate profit margins and equity valuations. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days when INDPRO accelerates.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 119.657 as of Dec 2010 reflects post-recession industrial recovery
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or months with INDPRO growth > 0.5% fail to produce positive 5-day returns in > 52% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) exceeding 0.3% signals inflation acceleration that threatens real returns and forces the Federal Reserve toward tightening, compressing equity valuations through higher discount rates. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days when inflation crosses this threshold.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle driven by inflation concerns
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL rises > 0.3% month-over-month, 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 5.5% and remains elevated for two consecutive months, it signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and spending power, producing negative S&P 500 returns. This regime-conditional mechanism is active only in post-crisis recovery phases where labor slack remains elevated.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.0% as of Jan 2011 indicates improving labor conditions
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with persistent labor market slack
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 5.5% for 2+ months, 20-day forward returns are negative in < 45% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint acceleration in real GDP growth (GDPC1) and retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust aggregate demand expansion that supports equity earnings growth and risk appetite. This interaction-driven mechanism requires both demand components to strengthen simultaneously to activate the bullish signal over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth > 2.5% AND RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.4%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11092.0B and RSAFS at 357013M as of Q4 2010
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with synchronized demand acceleration
failure_condition: When both GDPC1 growth > 2.5% and RSAFS growth > 0.4% occur together, 60-day forward returns are negative in > 45% of instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises simultaneously, it signals a stagflationary supply shock that compresses equity valuations through margin compression and recession risk. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days when both conditions activate.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month decline > 0.5% AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 119.657 and UNRATE at 5.0% as of Jan 2011
regime_hint: supply-side shock with labor market deterioration
failure_condition: When INDPRO declines > 0.5% and UNRATE rises > 0.2pp simultaneously, 20-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).
